title: Wedelia reflexa
taxonomy: Wedelia reflexa is classified under the family Asteraceae, which is commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. It belongs to the genus Wedelia and is a species of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of the Americas. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes over 23,000 species. This classification places Wedelia reflexa within the larger group of composites, which are characterized by their flower heads composed of multiple florets. The specific classification of Wedelia reflexa is Wedelia reflexa (Wedelia) or Achyranthes reflexa (some other sources)
uses: Wedelia reflexa is known for its medicinal properties, including its use in treating various health issues. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and folk medicine to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections. Its leaves and flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties. Wedelia reflexa is also believed to possess antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing. Furthermore, the plant is said to have potential applications in the treatment of diabetes and other metabolic disorders.

side_effects: Wedelia reflexa, also known as the gray signal plant, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. However, its use has also been associated with certain side effects.
